'''DS-181-3''' was the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. After receiving training at the [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt]] [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]] from [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]], DS-181-3 flew as Fel's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] in [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]], part of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]], and was given the nickname "'''Fel's Wrath'''." In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], DS-181-3 was present for [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] arrival aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]], and he later participated in the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Biography==
[[File:Saber3-CCGDS2.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Saber 3, the TIE/In Interceptor flown by DS-181-3]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="CCG1" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Callsign/Legends|designated]] DS-181-3 was a [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Studying under renowned pilot [[Baron/Legends|Baron]] [[Soontir Fel/Legends|Soontir Fel]] at the [[Prefsbelt IV/Legends|Prefsbelt]] [[Imperial Naval Academy/Legends|Imperial Academy]], DS-181-3 went on to serve as Fel's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingman]] in [[Saber Squadron (Imperial)/Legends|Saber Squadron]]—part of the [[181st Imperial Fighter Wing/Legends|181st Imperial Fighter Group]]—piloting the [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE/IN interceptor]] [[Saber 3]].<ref name="CCG1" /> Regularly ordered to remain at a distance from Fel to allow the Baron room to maneuver during [[Starfighter combat/Legends|combat]],<ref name="CCG2">{{CCG|set=Death Star II Limited|cardname=Saber 3}}</ref> DS-181-3 was given the nickname "Fel's Wrath."<ref name="CSWE" />

In [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] to the beginning of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> DS-181-3 was present when [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] arrived aboard the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] to discuss the [[Battle station/Legends|battle station's]] lagging construction schedule with the station's commander, [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Tiaan Jerjerrod/Legends|Tiaan Jerjerrod]]. The Sith Lord announced that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] himself would be arriving to personally oversee the remainder of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star's]] assembly.<ref name="ROTJ">{{Film|VI}}</ref> DS-181-3 later flew on Fel's wing during the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]],<ref name="CSWE" /> which ended in an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] defeat and the destruction of the second Death Star.<ref name="CSWE-Battle of Endor">{{CSWECite|I|235–236|Endor, Battle of}}</ref>

==Personality and traits==
Trained by the Empire's most successful pilot, Baron Soontir Fel,<ref name="CSWE" /> DS-181-3 earned a place as Fel's wingman in Saber Squadron. In combat, DS-181-3 would watch the Baron's back,<ref name="CCG1" /> though he was often ordered to remain at a distance to allow Fel to maneuver.<ref name="CCG2" /> DS-181-3 had [[Color/Legends|black]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and light skin.<ref name="CCG1" />

==Equipment==
During his Imperial service, DS-181-3 wore a black [[Imperial Navy crewman]]'s [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]].<ref name="CCG1" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:MoffJerjerrodGreetsVader.jpg|thumb|right|250px|The scene from the ''Special Edition'' of ''The Empire Strikes Back'' that was reused from Vader's arrival on the Death Star in ''Return of the Jedi'']]
DS-181-3 first appeared in the opening scene of [[1983]]'s [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] as an unnamed background character awaiting the arrival of Darth Vader on the second Death Star.<ref name="ROTJ" /> In [[2000]], he was first identified by name on a card for the ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]],<ref name="CCG1" /> with the name simultaneously being used as a gameplay statistic for his call sign.<ref name="CCG2" />

Because part of the scene showing Vader's arrival on the second Death Star in ''Return of the Jedi'' was reused in the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|''Special Edition'']] release of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']]<ref name="BTS note">{{DB|character|moffjerjerrod|Jerjerrod, Moff}}</ref> for a scene showing Vader arriving aboard the ''Executor'' from Bespin, the actor who portrayed DS-181-3 can be seen in the ''Special Edition'' version of ''The Empire Strikes Back'', alongside other extras who portrayed Imperial officers in ''Return of the Jedi''.<ref name="ESB">{{Film|V}}</ref>
